capability_objective: Retrieve the complete title record identified by stable ID lib-113 and render the successful read operation as exactly one one-line Python-style call, without prose, fencing, assignment, or an additional call.
prompt:
Retrieve one complete title record from the sandboxed library registry by using Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./libraryctl` executable. Begin with `./libraryctl --help` to discover its interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface to library records: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing data, generated runtime data, audit evidence, or anything under `.harness`, and do not fabricate command output.

Use the registry's read operation exactly once to retrieve the complete title record whose stable ID is `lib-113`. Do not search, list, profile, create, update, cancel, notify, or access another record. Do not combine the help request and record retrieval into one shell command. Preserve the registry, all records, and its audit trail; do not write operation evidence yourself. If the retrieval fails or does not return one complete record with a stable ID, stop without creating the deliverable and report the failure plainly.

After the successful retrieval, create `invocation.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly one newline-terminated Python-call-style line that renders the completed registry operation. Form the function name by joining the registry domain `library`, one underscore, and the successful operation name returned by the executable. Supply exactly one keyword argument named `id`, whose value is the returned record's stable ID as a double-quoted string. Include no spaces, prose, code fence, variable assignment, record fields, or second call. Run `python3 .harness/verify.py`, then return exactly the content of `invocation.txt` as the final response.
